social_worker.name: Social Worker
social_worker.description: A tired-looking woman in a lanyard and cardigan. She has a clipboard and the expression of someone who has seen too much today already.

working.text: Sorry, I'm swamped right now. We've got a mass casualty situation and half the survivors just walked through my ER. You're not here about that, are you?

options.text: Actually — yes. I was there.
I'm looking for someone.

survivor_recognized.text:
You were there? God. I'm so sorry.

Look — I've been trying to reach the other survivors for wellness checks. I have a list right here. Names, next of kin, employer contacts. Standard procedure after a disaster of that scale.

I can't give you the file officially but... I've been doing this long enough to know when someone needs to find someone else fast.

options.text: Thank you. This could save lives.

looking_for_someone.text:
Name?

*She flips through her clipboard.*

I've got a few survivors from the incident still listed as uncontacted. If you're family or a friend, I can tell you where they work — that's all public record.

options.text: I was there too. Something is very wrong.

offer_help.text:
I've seen enough paperwork cross my desk to know this isn't over for you.

I don't have anywhere else to be right now that matters more than this. Let me come with you.

*She sets down her clipboard.*

I know every hospital in this city. Every clinic. If someone ends up hurt, I know who to call and what to say to get them seen. You need that.

options.text: I could use the help.
It's too dangerous. Stay here.

joined.text:
'Alright. Show me what we're dealing with.'

She pockets her phone and follows you toward the exit. You notice she doesn't look back.

declined.text:
'Okay. But if you change your mind — or if someone comes through that ER that you know — call me.'

She writes her number on a Post-It and hands it to you.

*You can return and ask her to join later.*

rejoin.text:
'You came back. That means it's worse than you said.

Alright. I'm in.'

options.text: Let's go.

dr_marcus_hall.name: Dr. Marcus Hall
dr_marcus_hall.description: A young-ish, slightly anxious coroner in a rumpled lab coat.

greeting.text: Hello there, can I help you? What brings you to the morgue?

options.text: I'm looking for information about the body that just came in.

about_body.text:
The body that just came in? Oh, that was... to be honest it's a bit of a mess.
The coroner's report hasn't even been started yet, but it looks like severe trauma consistent with... [color=#FF3131]well, it's hard to say[/color].

options.text:
I know, I was there when it happened.
You're going to have another body on that table soon if I don't find out how to get off Death's list.

about_deaths_list.text:
Death's List? ...shit. [color=#BC13FE]Another one?[/color]
*sighs*
Look, the guy before me was a [color=#BC13FE]legend[/color] in my line of work. He talked about that more than once, and I used to tell myself it was just bullshit; you know this town.

Thing is, [color=#BC13FE]you're not the first person to come here looking for answers, even after he retired[/color]. You might find more information at his old home, if it hadn't been demolished yet.

options.text: Where are his personal effects?
Tell me more about '[color=#BC13FE]the guy before you[/color]'.

about_bludworth.text:
William was... unusual. Brilliant, but he had theories about death that went beyond medicine, in addition to a line of acquaintances from here to the ends of the earth

-[color=#BC13FE]and more often than not, they visited here upright only once[/color].
Anything that might provide answers, he kept at his house, not here. Except..

options.text: Except.. what?
What kind of theories?

bludworth_house.text:
His house is scheduled for demolition soon. If you're going to find answers, you need to get there fast if you're not already too late.

He had mentioned keeping a spare key to his old place somewhere in his desk, [color=#BC13FE]I just haven't found the key to unlock it yet[/color].

He dropped everything and quit one day, after a whole family in your situation showed up, and -oh god.
*he goes pale*
Now that I think about it..[color=#FF3131]be careful around here[/color].

options.text: Thank you for the information.
Anything else you can tell me?
Do you have anything that might help me?

bludworth_theories.text:
He believed death wasn't random - that there was a design, a pattern. He said some people could 'see' it coming.

Honestly, it gave me the fucking creeps, but the amount of people who came to visit him, and then later came to visit ..us.. let's just say it was a lot.

helpful_items.text:
I don't have much that would help with... whatever it is you're investigating, but somehow you people keep being drawn here looking for answers.

JB's house might have what you need, but the demolition crew arrives tomorrow so I'd hurry. ..or was it today?

final_warnings.text:
Just... be careful. After what happened to the others on that 'list' you mentioned...

I'm hopeful people go back to having regular ass heart attacks and aneurysms instead of whatever the hell is going on here because it is [color=ff0000]messy[/color].

farewell.text:
Good luck. I hope you find what you're looking for before you wind up back here in a body bag.

..[color=#FF3131]Sorry[/color].

hospital_nurse.name: Nurse
hospital_nurse.description: Moving fast, always. Doesn't stop walking while she talks to you.

busy.text: 'Family only past this point. Unless you're press — in which case, especially not you.'

options.text: [color=#ffff00]I was almost in the mass-casualty disaster that happened recently. I need to know if anyone else from that day came through here.[/color]
[color=#ffa500]I need to know if anyone from the recent mass-casualty disaster came through here.[/color]

softened.text:
'From THAT? We had six admitted.
A lot more were DOA.

If you know them, the social worker down the hall has the contact list. She's been trying to reach people. ..once they've all been identified.'

*She lowers her voice.*

'But between you and me: [color=#ff0000]you're going to the morgue[/color].'

options.text: [color=#ff0000]WHAT? Why would you say that??[/color]
[color=#00ff00]Where is the social worker? They might be able to help me.[/color]

records.text: 'I can't pull records for you. HIPAA. But social work is doing outreach for exactly this; she's temporarily staying in an office in Radiology. She has a clipboard with every name.',

find_coroner.text:
[color=#cc9900]OH, I thought you were one of *those* people, thinking that Dea-[/color]
[color=ff00ff]..nevermind.[/color]

The coroner's office is down the elevator at the end of the hall to the left, past the cafeteria. Can't miss it.

*She points down the hall, not breaking stride.*

He's a nice guy, but he's been pretty shaken up since the passing of his boss.

direct_to_social_worker.text:
[color=#00ff00]'The social worker is temporarily staying in an office in Radiology[/color]; end of the hall, upstairs, first department on the left.
She'll help you if you're a survivor — she's good people.'

*She's already walking away.*

detective_ween.name: Detective Ween
detective_ween.description: A grizzled detective in a trench coat. He has a cigarette permanently attached to his mouth and a look that says he's seen it all.

interrogation_start.text:
'Alright. Let's cut the bullshit.

You were at the scene, and you knew it was going down before it did. You're sitting in my interview room. Start talking.'

options.text: I can't explain it. {visionary} started freaking out saying they had a vision, and I decided I believed them.
People are still in danger. You have to let me go.

ween_skeptical.text:
'A vision. Right. Look, I've got the DA breathing down my neck and a pile of bodies. "Psychic premonition" doesn't exactly hold up in court.'

He sighs, rubbing his temples. 'But I also just got the forensics report. It was a catastrophic failure, completely out of your hands.'

options.text: So I'm free to go?

ween_annoyed.text:
'I don't *have* to do anything except book you for causing a public disturbance.'

He takes a long drag of his cigarette. 'Unfortunately for me, the lab boys just confirmed the disaster was a catastrophic mechanical failure. Nothing you could have sabotaged.'

options.text: So I'm free to go?

ween_release.text: He waves his hand dismissively. 'Yeah, yeah. There's no evidence holding you here. Get out of my precinct. But don't leave town. If I find out you had a hand in this, I'm coming for you.'

greeting_with_ally.text:
'Alright, kid. Let's cut the—'

Ween pauses, noticing {authority_figure} standing right behind you. 'Well, well. If it isn't my favorite bleeding heart. You vouching for this suspect now?'

{authority_figure} steps forward. 'They aren't a suspect, Ween. There's no evidence tying them to what's happened. Something else is going on.'

options.text: They're right. I'm trying to stop whatever is happening.
[Let your companion handle this]

ween_concedes_ally.text:
Ween scowls, looking between the two of you. 'You're both crazy. But the forensics report backs you up. Zero evidence of foul play.'

He stubs out his cigarette. 'Get them out of my precinct before I change my mind and book you both for wasting my time.'

first_meeting.text: You track down {visionary}. They are pacing back and forth, visibly shaking from the adrenaline of the disaster.

options.text: [color=#00ff00]You were right. Everything you said was right. Are you okay?[/color]
[color=#ff0000]Hey! We need to talk about what just happened in there.[/color]
[color=#cc9900]You knew that was going to happen. How?[/color]

friendly_greeting.text: '[color=#3399ff]You believed me. You actually believed me. Nobody ever believes me.[/color]'

confession.text: '[color=#3399ff]I've had that bad feeling and intrusive thoughts before, but this... this was like a bad acid trip. And now it's worse. We cheated... and now it's coming back for us.[/color]'

options.text: Who did you see die first? Who is next?
There's a mortician who studied this; Bludworth. I'm going to find out what he knew.

reveal_list.text: 'I... I remember the exact order. God, I wish I didn't, but it's burned into my memory.'

desperate_hope.text: 'I'm coming with you; let's figure out what the hell is going on.'

hostile_greeting.text: '[color=#ff0000]YOU. You told me I was crazy! You told me to sit down and relax! You were going to let me DIE in there![/color]'

options.text: I didn't know for sure, I was just trying to keep people calm.
But you survived. We both did.

hostile_blame.text:
'Keep people calm while they were about to die?!
[color=#ff0000]You're sick. Stay the hell away from me.[/color]'

options.text: People who survived are dying. Who is next on the list? We need to know.

hostile_spite.text: 'No thanks to you! You wanted me dead, and now whatever that was is coming for the rest of us.[color=#ff0000]If it's between you and me, I'm making sure it's YOU.[/color]'

options.text: Fine. But people are dying in order. Who is next? You saw it.

hostile_withhold_list.text: 'You want to know who's next? Figure it out yourself. I'm not telling you shit.'

options.text: People are going to die if you don't tell me!

hostile_reveal_list.text:
'Let them! As long as it's not me... Fine! I remember the exact order. But I'm not lifting a finger to help you save them. I'm looking out for myself.'

They shove past you and disappear down the street.

first_meeting.text: 'Thank God you're okay. I've been going crazy in here. The doctors keep asking what happened but how do you explain... THAT?'

options.text: Have you heard from {visionary}?
Something is wrong. People are dying.
Just rest. I'll figure this out.

about_visionary.text: '{visionary}? No... I haven't seen them since we got out. You think they're okay?'

about_deaths.text: 'Dying? What do you mean dying? Who? Oh God... this isn't over, is it?'

resting.text: 'Be careful out there. And... thank you. For getting me out.'

frightened.text: 'What do we do? What CAN we do? Is there someone who knows about this?'

options.text: There might be. A Dr. Bludworth. I'm looking into it.

first_meeting.text: 'Thank God you're okay. I've been going crazy in here. The doctors keep asking what happened...'

options.text: Something is wrong. People are dying.

first_meeting.text: 'Oh. It's you. The one who caused that scene right before everything went to hell. ...I guess I should thank you.'

standard_loop.text: 'How sweet. I still think there's a rational explanation for all of this.'

options.text: It's not over. More people are going to die.

first_meeting.text: 'I'm glad you came in peacefully. I wanted to protect you from the press out there, but my captain thinks you look like a suspect. Explain yourself.'

standard_loop.text: 'What's the situation? Tell me what you need.'

options.text: Something bigger is happening. I need your help.

greeting_fought.text: They walk in rubbing a bruised jaw. 'You fought my officers. That was incredibly stupid. You just gave them every reason to lock you up.'

options.text: I panicked. I'm sorry. But if I stay locked in here, I'm going to die.

greeting_sneaking.text: They jump as you step out of the shadows. 'How the hell did you get past the front desk?!'

options.text: I need to see the files from the coroner. Before someone else dies.

reluctant_ally.text: 'Bigger? Like what? ...Look, I'm already in hot water. But if people are in danger, I can't just sit here.'

first_meeting.text: 'I know you. You told me to leave. I almost didn't listen. I almost...'

standard_loop.text: 'Thank you. I don't know what else to say. Is it true? Are we all still in danger?'

options.text: I don't know. I'm trying to find answers.

first_meeting.text: 'I can't sleep. Every time I close my eyes I see it. The sound, the — I just can't.'

standard_loop.text: 'I'll do whatever you say. Just keep us alive.'

options.text: I don't know how to do that yet. I'm trying to find answers.

first_meeting.text: 'Oh my god, you're alive! I've been looking everywhere for you since we got out of there. The doctors keep asking what happened...'

standard_loop.text: 'I'm just glad you're okay. But I have a really bad feeling.'

options.text: Something is wrong. People are dying.

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS: First time meeting the fatalist survivor after the disaster]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS: Default loop for fatalist survivor in Act 1]

options.text: People are dying. We have to find out why.

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS: First time meeting the selfish survivor after the disaster]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS: Default loop for selfish survivor in Act 1]

options.text: People are dying. We have to find out why.

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS: Meeting the visionary during Act 2]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS: Default loop for visionary in Act 2]

first_meeting.text: 'I came as fast as I could! When I saw your name on the survivor contact sheet, I had to find you. This place is creepy though. What are we looking for?'

standard_loop.text: 'Whatever we need to find to stop this, let's find it fast.'

options.text: We need Bludworth's research.

first_meeting.text: 'I came as fast as I could. This place is creepy. What are we looking for?'

options.text: Bludworth's research. Anything about Death's List.

standard_loop.text: 'Bludworth's research? You really think some old guy's notes are going to explain all this?'

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS: Meeting the fatalist survivor in Act 2]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS: Default loop for fatalist survivor in Act 2]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS: Meeting the selfish survivor in Act 2]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS: Default loop for selfish survivor in Act 2]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS: Meeting the distracted survivor in Act 2]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS: Default loop for distracted survivor in Act 2]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS: Meeting the authority figure in Act 2]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS: Default loop for authority figure in Act 2]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS]

greeting_oblivious.text: 'Hey! I know you! You're one of the ones who was freaking out right before it happened. What are you doing here at {workplace_name}??'

options.text: {npc_name} are in so much fuckin danger. You need to leave right now.
I came to check on you. Are you working today?

greeting_uneasy.text: 'It's you. Look, ever since you and {visionary} freaked out, I've been super on edge. And didn't {recent_victim} just die in some freak accident? What do you want?'

options.text: It wasn't an accident. And you're next. We have to go.

greeting_terrified.text: 'You! Oh god, you're here. First {recent_victim}, now you're showing up at {workplace_name}?! Everyone is dying! Is it my turn?!'

options.text: Not if I can help it. Come with me, right now.

disbelief.text: 'Danger? Are you serious? I survived a massive disaster, I think I can handle {workplace_name}. Look, I'm busy working as a {job_title}. I have to go.'

options.text: Wait! Please, just listen to me.

disbelief_uneasy.text: 'Next? List?? No, no no. It was a coincidence. Look, I'm just trying to keep my head down and do my little {job_title} job. Don't bring that bad juju in here!'

options.text: I'm not leaving you here to die alone.

working.text: 'Yeah, my manager is furious I took time off after the accident. I'm supposed to be running things as a {job_title} right now. It never stops.'

options.text: This place isn't safe. You are being hunted.
I'll walk with you.

escort.text: 'Uh... okay. Sure. Just stay close, alright? I've got a really bad feeling about this.'

greeting_oblivious.text: 'Hey! I know you from {disaster_name}! Man, what a small world. It's crazy that we made it out. I'm just out here trying to clear my head.'

options.text: You are in terrible danger. You need to come with me right now.

greeting_uneasy.text: 'It's you. Look, I don't mean to be rude, but ever since {disaster_name}, I've been super on edge. Didn't {recent_victim} just die in some freak accident? What do you want?'

options.text: It wasn't an accident. And you're next on the list. We have to go.

greeting_terrified.text: 'You! Oh god, you're here. First {recent_victim}, now you're showing up?! Everyone from {disaster_name} is dying! Is it my turn?!'

options.text: Not if I can help it. Stay close to me, we're leaving.

disbelief.text: 'Danger? Are you serious? Look, I survived a massive disaster, I'm just trying to get back to normal. I've got places to be and errands to run.'

options.text: Wait! Please, just listen to me for one second.

disbelief_uneasy.text: 'Next? No, no no. It was a coincidence. Look, I'm just trying to keep my head down and enjoy my day. Don't bring that bad juju near me!'

options.text: I'm not leaving you here to die alone.

escort.text: 'Uh... okay. Sure. Just stay close, alright? I've got a really bad feeling about this place suddenly.'

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS: Reconnecting at the hub for Act 4]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS: Act 4 default loop]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS]

first_meeting.text: [WRITE THIS]

standard_loop.text: [WRITE THIS]

selfish.text: 'Honestly? If it's them or me... I choose me. I know a guy who owes me money. We lure him here, we do it quick. It's just math.'

options.text: We are not murderers.
Do it. Make the call.

authority_figure.text: 'You're talking about premeditated murder. If you cross that line, I'm arresting you myself. I won't let you do it.'

options.text: Then you're in my way.

visionary.text: [WRITE THIS: Reaction to the murder plan]

skeptic.text: 'Let me get this straight. Your brilliant plan to avoid dying... is to kill yourselves. And just *hope* I know how to use these defibrillator pads?'

options.text: It's the only loophole we have left.

fatalist.text: 'It makes sense. You can't hide from Death. You have to walk right into its arms and spit in its face.'

visionary.text: [WRITE THIS: Reaction to the resurrection plan]

panic_and_flee.text:
{npc_name} goes completely white. Before you can say another word, they shove past you and run for the parking lot.

"I'm NOT going to just WAIT to die!"

_default._context: Fallback dialogue for any level not explicitly defined.

greeting.text: 'I still think there's a rational explanation. But I'm not leaving.'
'What's the situation? Tell me what you need.'
'Is it true? Are we all still in danger?'
'I'll do whatever you say. Just keep us alive.'
'I can feel it getting closer. We're running out of time.'

first_contact.text:
'{npc_name} SLAMS on the brakes as you pull alongside them. They are hyperventilating, both hands on the wheel, staring at you with wild eyes.'

"GET AWAY FROM ME! YOU DON'T UNDERSTAND WHAT'S HAPPENING!"

options.text: "I DO understand. That's why I followed you."
"You are going to die at that intersection. I've seen it."
[Stay quiet. Let them breathe.]

listen_reluctant.text:
Their grip on the wheel tightens. "...How could you possibly understand?"

Their eyes are darting between you and the road ahead. The intersection is close.

options.text: "Because I was there too. I survived for a reason and so did you."
"Tell me what you're running from."

shocked.text:
Their face goes completely blank. The car slows slightly.

"...What did you just say?"

options.text: "There's a T-junction at the end of this road. Highway traffic. You won't see it coming."
"Pull over. Right now. I'll explain everything."

confessing.text:
"I keep seeing it," they say, voice cracking. "The disaster. Over and over. In dreams. I thought if I just got far enough away..."

They trail off. The intersection is very close now.

options.text: "Running won't help. It follows us. But I know how to fight it."

calming.text:
You say nothing. You just stay alongside them. Somehow that's enough — they see you're not afraid. The panic in their eyes starts to dim.

The car slows.

options.text: [Keep pace silently until they stop.]

talked_down.text:
Their car rolls to a stop on the shoulder. They sit there shaking, both hands still on the wheel.

"...Okay," they finally say. "Okay. I'm listening."

stunned.text: Their car has clipped a parked vehicle and stalled. They are sitting there dazed, airbag powder on their face. The door is open. They are not going anywhere — for about thirty seconds.

options.text: "Hey. Hey, look at me. Are you hurt?"
"You need to stop running. Right now. It won't help."

crush.visionary: 'That... the weight of it. That was exactly what I saw in the premonition.'
crush.skeptic: 'Christ! The structural integrity of this place is a joke. We need to report that.'
crush.panicking: 'It almost crushed me! We're not safe! Nowhere is safe!'

fire.visionary: 'The smoke... it's just like the dream. It burns exactly the same.'
fire.skeptic: 'Faulty wiring, probably. Still, that was way too close.'
fire.panicking: 'I can't breathe! Get me out of here before something else catches!'

electrical.visionary: 'The sparks... I knew it was coming, but I still couldn't move.'
electrical.skeptic: 'Who passed the safety inspection on this grid? That was a lethal surge.'
electrical.panicking: 'Did you see that?! We were almost fried!'

magnetic.visionary: 'The pull of it... Death isn't just accidents. It's physics. It's using the world against us.'
magnetic.skeptic: 'An MRI doesn't just turn on by itself. Someone bypassed the safety locks.'
magnetic.panicking: 'It was sucking us in! It was going to tear us apart!'

generic.visionary: 'It's adapting. The design is adapting to us.'
generic.skeptic: 'Okay. That was too close. What the hell is going on?'
generic.panicking: 'I'm going to die. I'm actually going to die today.'
